budgetEvidence.hardwareTimingRationale: NTSC NES provides one physical frame per gameplay tick, while bounded packed-column work at a non-zero vertical camera offset must remain safely staged across NMI windows.
budgetEvidence.productionTraceRationale: The exact production source removes input, sprites, audio, collision, and gameplay while retaining the full collision-free stage1 visual fixture, the accepted one-pixel cadence, and the runner-shaped Y=80 camera offset. The bottom-aligned checkpoint is physical frame 104, after the declared 64-gameplay-tick framing delay; frame 40 is still inside cartridge preload/framing. The focused X=96 limit makes the retained window cover rightward streaming, reversal, and return, exposing both the ten-tile vertical misprojection and physical 30-row attribute-seam corruption without any adjacent gameplay subsystem.
